claim: UNESCO reports Sub-Saharan Africa has highest gender gap in secondary education, 43% girls vs 50% boys completing lower secondary, rates below 20% in Sahel, and identifies school fees, early marriage, and safety as key barriers.

source.title: Global Education Monitoring Report 2023: Gender Report

method: Reviewed GEM Gender Report chapters on Sub-Saharan Africa, enrollment trends, and barrier analysis.
limitations: Completion data is modeled from surveys of variable recency.
Barrier classification is qualitative and may not capture local variation.

claim: A 2020 systematic review found eliminating school fees, cash transfers, and school safety improvements had strongest evidence for increasing girls secondary enrollment, but effect sizes varied by context.

source.title: Improving girls secondary education outcomes in low-income countries: a systematic review

method: Reviewed systematic search, meta-analysis methods, and intervention effect sizes.
limitations: Effect sizes vary substantially by context and outcome measure.
Many studies are from East Africa, with fewer from West and Central Africa.
